news.title: Titan reaches an all-time high amid a rising market trend.

news.title:
Titan shares hit record high at ₹4,470 on 6% surge post strong Q4 update.
Domestic business grew 42% YoY; international operations soared 156% YoY in FY26 Q4.
news.summary:
Titan shares hit record high at ₹4,470 on 6% surge post strong Q4 update.
Domestic business grew 42% YoY; international operations soared 156% YoY in FY26 Q4.

news.title:
Titan Company sees breakout above resistance with a bullish weekly candle.
Stock trades above 20-week SMA, upper Bollinger Band, showing uptrend.
news.summary:
Titan Company sees breakout above resistance with a bullish weekly candle.
Stock trades above 20-week SMA, upper Bollinger Band, showing uptrend.

news.title:
Titan shares rose 3.7% on BSE after a strong Q4FY26 update; jewellery biz grew 46% YoY.
Watch sales grew 7% YoY, eyecare 16%, fragrances 30%, but Taneira declined 1% YoY.
news.summary:
Titan shares rose 3.7% on BSE after a strong Q4FY26 update; jewellery biz grew 46% YoY.
Watch sales grew 7% YoY, eyecare 16%, fragrances 30%, but Taneira declined 1% YoY.

news.title:
Titan Co. Ltd. witnessed an NSE block trade worth Rs. 22.1 cr.
The trade involved 50,352 shares at Rs. 4,394.4 per share.
news.summary:
Titan Co. Ltd. witnessed an NSE block trade worth Rs. 22.1 cr.
The trade involved 50,352 shares at Rs. 4,394.4 per share.

news.title:
Titan added 170 stores in Q4FY26, expanding to 3,603 stores, with 128 international additions via Damas integration.
Brokerages highlight strong jewellery growth but flag margin risks; Morgan Stanley targets Rs 4,529, Citigroup Rs 4,750.
news.summary:
Titan added 170 stores in Q4FY26, expanding to 3,603 stores, with 128 international additions via Damas integration.
Brokerages highlight strong jewellery growth but flag margin risks; Morgan Stanley targets Rs 4,529, Citigroup Rs 4,750.

news.title: Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight rating on Titan with TP of 4,529.
news.summary: Morgan Stanley maintains Overweight rating on Titan with TP of 4,529.

news.title:
Goldman Sachs recommends buying Titan with a target price of ₹5,000.
Jewellery segment grew 52% YoY, with strong international sales despite West Asia challenges.
news.summary:
Goldman Sachs recommends buying Titan with a target price of ₹5,000.
Jewellery segment grew 52% YoY, with strong international sales despite West Asia challenges.

news.title:
Brokerages focus on Titan, DLF, Hindalco, and affordable housing NBFCs, flagging rural demand risks and margin compression.
Top picks include Bharti Airtel, Tata Steel, Home First Finance, and premium consumer plays like Titan and HUL.
news.summary:
Brokerages focus on Titan, DLF, Hindalco, and affordable housing NBFCs, flagging rural demand risks and margin compression.

news.title:
Kaustubh Pawaskar discusses Trent, Marico, and Titan's outlook amid monsoon uncertainty.
Focus remains on FMCG performance as weather conditions may impact demand and operations.
news.summary:
Kaustubh Pawaskar discusses Trent, Marico, and Titan's outlook amid monsoon uncertainty.

news.title:
Titan's domestic business grew 42% YoY with 42 net new stores.
Jewellery grew 46% YoY, watches 7% YoY, and international surged 156% YoY.

news.title:
Retail sector saw 23% YoY revenue growth in Q4 FY26, driven by jewellery demand.
HSBC analysts cite strong SSSG in jewellery; sector resilient to rising input costs.
news.summary:
Retail sector saw 23% YoY revenue growth in Q4 FY26, driven by jewellery demand.

news.title:
Eternal, Titan, JSW Steel, and Tata Steel are set to post over 50% YoY Q4 profit growth.
MOSL sees Eternal's PAT soaring 432%, Titan's PAT rising 58.7%, and strong numbers for steel firms.
news.summary:
Eternal, Titan, JSW Steel, and Tata Steel are set to post over 50% YoY Q4 profit growth.

news.title:
MOFSL sees FY27 as favorable for Indian equities post FY26 underperformance caused by FII outflows and geopolitical risks.
Top stock picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M, Titan, Infosys, and TVS Motors; mid-caps outperform large-caps.
news.summary:
MOFSL sees FY27 as favorable for Indian equities post FY26 underperformance caused by FII outflows and geopolitical risks.
Top stock picks include Bharti Airtel, SBI, ICICI Bank, M&M, Titan, Infosys, and TVS Motors; mid-caps outperform large-caps.

news.title:
Titan launches 'Hues' with 200+ coloured gemstone designs under Tanishq to target millennials.
With 87% of Q3 revenue from jewellery, Titan bets on sub-₹1L segment amid rising gold prices.

news.title:
Analysts recommend Adani Power, Titan, Cummins India, Mankind Pharma, and Arvind Ltd. based on technical patterns and sectoral momentum.
Targets include Adani Power at Rs 170, Titan at Rs 4,180, Cummins India at Rs 4,820, Mankind Pharma at Rs 2,250, and Arvind Ltd. at Rs 430.

news.title:
Titan, Adani Power, and GPIL are bullish picks; Titan targets Rs. 4,500, Adani Power Rs. 180, GPIL Rs. 298.
Titan shows a breakout; Adani Power confirms uptrend; GPIL breaks triangle, RSI above 60 for all stocks.

news.title:
Motilal Oswal highlights top investment themes amid market volatility, citing geopolitical and economic challenges like the Iran-Israel conflict and rupee depreciation.
Top stock picks include SBI, ICICI Bank, BEL, IndiGo, Titan, TVS Motor, Groww, and Dixon Tech; Nifty 50 offers value at a 15% P/E discount.
news.summary:
Motilal Oswal highlights top investment themes amid market volatility, citing geopolitical and economic challenges like the Iran-Israel conflict and rupee depreciation.
Top stock picks include SBI, ICICI Bank, BEL, IndiGo, Titan, TVS Motor, Groww, and Dixon Tech; Nifty 50 offers value at a 15% P/E discount.

news.title:
ICRA reaffirmed Titan's ratings, assigning AAA (Stable) for Rs. 6,200 crore and Rs. 1,000 crore instruments.
Enhanced fund-based/non-fund-based working capital facilities rated AAA (Stable) for Rs. 14,750 crore, up from Rs. 7,305 crore.
news.summary:
ICRA reaffirmed Titan's ratings, assigning AAA (Stable) for Rs. 6,200 crore and Rs. 1,000 crore instruments.
Enhanced fund-based/non-fund-based working capital facilities rated AAA (Stable) for Rs. 14,750 crore, up from Rs. 7,305 crore.
